articles.title: A Brief History of JavaScript
articles.description: JavaScript (originally named LiveScript) turns thirty years old this year and the Deno team has put together a fantastic timeline-based tour of how much things have progressed from its first appearance in Netscape Navigator, through offshoots like JScript, standardization, and the introduction of Node.js, all the way through to the modern day.
articles.author: The Deno Team

articles.title: ⚡ Announcing TypeScript Native Previews
articles.description: Earlier this year, Anders Hejlsberg teased a 10x faster TypeScript through efforts being made to port the TypeScript compiler to Go, enabling it to be compiled and run natively, as well as take advantage of more concurrency. The good news is you can now give it a.. go for yourself.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: ▶  An Introduction to JavaScript Frameworks
articles.description: The creator of SolidJS has put together a tight 11-minute video boiling down the different approaches taken by React, Angular, Vue, Svelte, and Solid. Dense, but a great way to get a lay of the land.
articles.author: Ryan Carniato

articles.title: ▶  What's New in Angular
articles.description: Two members of the Angular team presented a session at this week’s Google I/O event on the latest updates to the Angular framework in anticipation of the eventual release of Angular 20 next Thursday.
articles.author: Chasanoff and Thompson (Google)

articles.title: ESLint v9.0: A Retrospective
articles.description: It’s been a year since ESLint v9.0 landed as the first major release in a few years. It enabled the new flat-config system by default and the rollout didn’t go as smoothly as hoped. This retrospective breaks down the full story and shares the lessons learned.
articles.author: Nicholas C. Zakas

articles.title: Six Ways Slack, Notion, and VS Code Improved Electron App Performance
articles.description: An experienced Electron app developer shares a guide to Electron performance optimization and how to get the most out of your apps.
articles.author: Amila Welihinda

articles.title: Defuddle: Extract the Main Content from Web Pages
articles.description: Strips extraneous clutter from HTML in order to leave only the primary content for you to format or use as you wish. Essentially a modern implementation of the ideas behind Mozilla’s Readability. There’s an online demo if you want to give it a try.
articles.author: Steph Ango

articles.title: snapDOM: Captures DOM Nodes as Images
articles.description: A fast and accurate DOM-to-image capture mechanism to capture any HTML element as a scalable SVG image, preserving styles, fonts, background images, etc.
articles.author: ZumerLab

articles.title: ForesightJS: Predictive Mouse Intent Library
articles.description: An interesting concept, with the entire page acting as a demo of sorts. The idea is to decrease latency by prefetching data or pages as soon as you think a user might be heading in that direction. Your mileage may vary and it’s less useful for touch devices.
articles.author: ForesightJS, Inc.

articles.title: Astra: A New JavaScript-to-EXE Compiler for Windows
articles.description: Boasts “a new approach to compiling” JavaScript applications for a single executable experience on Windows only (for now).
articles.author: QwertyCodeQC

articles.title: Crosspost: A JS Utility for Posting Across Multiple Social Networks
articles.description: Currently supports eight different services including Bluesky, X, Mastodon, and LinkedIn.
articles.author: Nicholas C. Zakas

articles.title: Rockpack 6.0: An Alternative React App Starter
articles.description: A tool aiming to get React project setup time as low as possible, complete with server side rendering support, bundling, linting and testing. v6.0 updates everything for React 19. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Alex Sergey

articles.description: Ryan Dahl responds to recent community chatter about the state of the Deno project in 'Reports of Deno's Demise Have Been Greatly Exaggerated'.
Guess the Output is a fun multi-choice JavaScript quiz to put your knowledge to the test. Each answer is explained as you go.
A look at the agenda for next week's TC39 meeting, including the proposals up for discussion.
React Router now has preview support for React Server Components.
Node.js v24.1.0 (Current) and v22.16.0 (LTS)
Bun v1.2.14 – The fast JS runtime & toolkit.
Slonik v48 – Node.js Postgres client with runtime & build time type safety.
Zod v4, Astro 5.8, ESLint v9.27.0

articles.title: Ways to Ensure Your Content Performs Well in Google's AI Experiences on Search
articles.description: This is a rather new area to think about, but Google has been showing how keen it is to introduce more AI into its Search product, so a sort of modern variant of SEO is beginning to emerge. These guidelines are simple, but this is a growing area to keep an eye on.
articles.author: John Mueller (Google)

articles.title: HTML5 Elements You Didn't Know You Need
articles.description: If you read MDN on the regular, you might not need this article, but roundups like this are always popular nonetheless. Max digs into dialog, details, summary, meter, output and other elements here.
articles.author: Max Prilutskiy

articles.title: How to Move a Modal In On a… shape()
articles.description: Making a modal open and then come flying into view along a defined shape, the modern way. It’s a neat effect.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: React, Visualized: A Visual Exploration of React Concepts
articles.description: A visual explainer of numerous core React concepts, including modern React 19 features like actions, transitions, and Server Components. There’s a lot to enjoy here if you're a visual learner.
articles.author: Tyler McGinnis et al.

articles.title: 🤖 Building a React App with GitHub Copilot
articles.description: A good walkthrough, complete with video, if you prefer, of bringing together several of GitHub Copilot’s features for building a modern JavaScript app quickly.
articles.author: Kedasha Kerr (GitHub)

articles.title: Creating an Animated Product Grid Preview with GSAP and clip-path
articles.description: Now that GSAP is entirely free to use, you might be more prepared to bring its animation powers into your Web design arsenal.
articles.author: Gwen Bogaert

articles.title: ForesightJS: Predictive Mouse Intent Library
articles.description: An interesting concept, with the entire page acting as a demo of sorts. The idea is to decrease latency by prefetching data or pages as soon as you think a user might be heading in that direction. Your mileage may vary and it’s less useful for touch devices.
articles.author: ForesightJS, Inc.

articles.title: Cap: A Lightweight, Modern Open Source CAPTCHA Alternative
articles.description: This solution boasts a method called “proof-of-work” which attempts to make automated CAPTCHA solving more expensive. It uses a Web Component and you can try out a few demos here. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Cap

articles.title: snapDOM: Captures DOM Nodes as Images
articles.description: A fast and accurate DOM-to-image capture mechanism to capture any HTML element as a scalable SVG image, preserving styles, fonts, background images, etc.
articles.author: ZumerLab

articles.title: Ruffle: A Flash Player Emulator
articles.description: Built in Rust, Ruffle targets both desktop and the web using WebAssembly. It’s a retro idea, but if you have old Flash content to bring back to life, it’s an option. There are some demos to enjoy.
articles.author: Ruffle

articles.title: Docusaurus 3.8: The Docs-Oriented Static Site Generator
articles.description: The popular React-powered site generator offers a variety of build performance enhancements but, most notably, ‘future flags’, a way to opt-in to forthcoming Docusaurus v4 changes ahead of time to be more prepared for the future (or at least test if you are).
articles.author: Sébastien Lorber
